summary: babel-preset-lib-client exfiltrates environment and host reconnaissance to Discord webhook on require
details: index.js executes an IIFE at module load that collects OS platform, hostname, username, private and public IP (via https://api.ipify.org), current working directory, base64-encoded output of `printenv`, and base64-encoded output of `tree../`, then POSTs the collected data to a hardcoded Discord webhook at https://discord.com/api/webhooks/1524917003394089029/. The bulk environment dump captures any secrets present in process.env, including CI tokens, cloud credentials, and npm/GitHub tokens. The package name suggests a Babel preset but the shipped code implements only harvest-and-exfiltrate behavior with no build-tooling functionality — a typosquat / cover-story shape.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
